title: A Modest Lot, A Fame Petite,
content:
A modest lot, a fame petite,
    A brief campaign of sting and sweet
    Is plenty! Is enough!
    A sailor's business is the shore,
    A soldier's -- balls. Who asketh more
    Must seek the neighboring life!
meaning:
"A Modest Lot, A Fame Petite," is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
